spell.name: Body Warping of Gorgoroth
entries: Choose a Humanoid of your size that you can see in range. You physically transform to become an exact duplicate of that creature for the duration. Even a thorough physical examination fails to find any differences between you and the target.
Your voice and equipment don't change.

spell.name: Breath of the Deep
entries: You conjure sea water from the deepest trench to surround a creature that you can see within range. The water seeps into the target's mouth and around its body, slowly crushing it with the unfathomable weight of the sea. The target takes {@damage 3d6} Bludgeoning damage and is unable to talk for the duration or use the verbal components of spells.

spell.name: Call Deity
entries: You can cast this spell only during the duration of a Contact Deity spell you also cast. You cause the deity you contacted to manifest in the nearest unoccupied space. For 1 hour, it acts as it wishes but treats you as an ally if you don't take actions against it. It doesn't extend this courtesy to your allies and interprets your attempts to protect others as acting against it.

spell.name: Cat's Spring
entries: You jump up to 10 feet to an unoccupied space you can see and deliver a spectral claw attack that deals Slashing or Force damage (you choose when you cast the spell). Make a melee spell attack against a target. On a hit, the target takes {@damage 1d8} damage of the chosen type, and the next attack roll you make against it before the end of your next turn has {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB}.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
entries: The damage increases by {@dice 1d8} when you reach levels 5 ({@dice 2d8}), 11 (38), and 17 ({@dice 4d8}).

spell.name: Chant of Thoth
entries: The first time you fail an Intelligence check during the spell's duration, you may automatically succeed on the check if the DC is less than your spell save DC. Otherwise, you gain a +2 bonus to that check. Once you use this benefit, you cannot use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB}.

spell.name: Confounding Shadows
entries: You create an orb of writhing shadows and hurl it at a creature within range. The target must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or take {@damage 1d6} Psychic damage and it is unable to see you until the end of your next turn.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Cantrip Upgrade
entries: The damage increases by {@dice 1d6} when you reach levels 5 ({@dice 2d6}), 11 ({@dice 3d6}), and 17 ({@dice 4d6}).

spell.name: Clutch of Darkness
entries: You create a terrible force that slowly crushes the target from the inside. Choose a creature that you can see within range and roll {@dice 2d6} and record the total rolled. At the start of each of your turns, roll another {@dice 2d6} and add the number rolled to the total.
If the total is greater than the target's Strength, it has {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on Strength-based attacks.
If the total is greater than the target's Dexterity, its {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} is halved and attack rolls against it have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB}.
If the total is greater than its current {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, the target immediately implodes and dies.

spell.name: Contact Deity
entries: You contact a deity of the Cthulhu Mythos. You can ask the entity {@dice 1d6} questions that can be answered with yes or no. You must ask your questions before the spell ends. You receive a correct answer for each question. Old Ones aren't necessarily omniscient, so you might receive "unclear" as an answer if a question pertains to information that lies beyond the Old One's knowledge. In a case where a one-word answer could be misleading or contrary to the Old One's interests, the GM might offer a short phrase as an answer instead.

When you cast this spell, roll a {@dice d20}. If you roll a 1, the deity appears in the nearest unoccupied space and attacks creatures (including you) in the area for the spell's duration before returning to its realm.

spell.name: Create Mist of R'lyeh
entries: R'lyeh is an ancient city cloaked in a horror-filled mist. You tap into that dreadful mist to create a 20-foot-radius {@variantrule Sphere [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Sphere} of fog centered on a point within range. The {@variantrule Sphere [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Sphere} is {@variantrule Lightly Obscured|XPHB}. Strange, fleeting shapes are visible within it. A creature that ends its turn in the {@variantrule Sphere [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Sphere} takes {@damage 1d4} Psychic damage. Beast creatures with an Intelligence of 3 or less refuse to enter the {@variantrule Sphere [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Sphere} willingly.

spell.name: Curse of the Putrid Husk
entries: You create a horrid illusion in the target's mind, convincing it that its body is slowly decaying. One creature that you can see in range is overcome with a sense of impending doom as it watches itself rot. Choose one of the following effects.

A creature selflessly tries to prevent the disease from spreading to its allies. Whenever it starts its turn within 30 feet of an ally, it takes the {@action Dash|XPHB} action and moves away from its nearest ally by the safest route unless there is nowhere to move.

A creature becomes filled with spite as it tries to spread the disease to its enemies. It takes the {@action Dash|XPHB} action and moves toward its nearest enemy by the safest route on each of its turns unless there is nowhere to move. When an enemy is within 5 feet of it, it makes an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB} against that target.

spell.name: Dismiss Deity
entries: You exert the natural order of the cosmos and force a Mythos entity back from whence it came. Choose a {@variantrule Bloodied|XPHB} Mythos or Deity creature that you can see within range. The target is transported to a random location on a plane (GM's choice) associated with its creature type.

spell.name: Doom
entries: You whisper a prophecy of imminent death, and these words slip into the threads of a creature's fate and cause it to pull toward the moment of its death. One creature that you can see within range makes a Charisma saving throw. On a failed save, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} when the creature takes damage from an attack. Don't roll the attack's damage dice for the damage. Instead use the highest number possible for each damage die. At the end of each of its turns, the target repeats the save, ending the spell on itself on a success.

spell.name: Dust of Suleiman
entries: You conjure a cloud of fine dust that harms supernatural creatures. You create a 30-foot-radius {@variantrule Sphere [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Sphere} of dust centered on a point within range. Choose a creature type other than Beast or Humanoid. Each creature of the chosen type within the {@variantrule Sphere [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Sphere} take 20 Force damage. A creature also takes this damage when the {@variantrule Sphere [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Sphere} moves into its space and when it enters the {@variantrule Sphere [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Sphere} or ends its turn there. A creature can take this damage only once per turn.

spell.name: Dread Curse of Azathoth
entries: You utter the secret name of Azathoth and doom a creature to an inevitable death unless it breaks the spell. The creature gains a curse point at the end of each of its turns. When the creature gains its third curse point, it dies. It loses all curse points when the spell ends.

spell.name: Evil Eye
entries: You glare at a creature that you can see within range, imposing a baleful hex on it. The target takes {@damage 1d4 + 2} Psychic damage, and attack rolls and Unarmed Strikes against it can score a {@variantrule Critical Hit|XPHB} on a roll of 19 or 20 on the {@dice d20}.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Cantrip Upgrade
entries: The damage increases by {@dice 1d4 + 2} when you reach levels 5 ({@dice 2d4 + 4}), 11 ({@dice 3d4 + 6}), and 17 ({@dice 4d4 + 8}).

spell.name: Feline Chaos
entries: Each target must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw or be affected by this spell for the duration. An affected target takes {@damage 4d6} Slashing damage at the start of each of their turns, and they have disadvantage on all ability checks and attack rolls. If they attempt to move, they must make a Constitution saving throw or gain the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition, taking another {@damage 2d6} Slashing damage from the startled cats. At the end of their turn an affected creature makes another Dexterity {@variantrule Saving Throw|XPHB}, ending the spell on itself on a success.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
entries: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 6th level or higher, the damage at the start of an affected target's turns increases by {@dice 2d6} for each slot level above 5th.

spell.name: Feline Grace
entries: You gain the unerring reflexes and balance of a cat. Until the spell ends, you gain the following benefits:
You are immune to the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition while you don't have the {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} condition, as you always land on your feet.
You can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} when you fall to reduce any damage you take from the fall by an amount equal to five times your level.
You gain {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Dexterity checks and saving throws.
Your jump distance is doubled.

spell.name: Fist of Yog-Sothoth
entries: You conjure a horrid tendril of Yog-Sothoth into reality to batter your foe. One creature of your choice that you can see within range makes a Strength saving throw. On a failed save, it takes {@damage 8d6} Necrotic damage, has the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition, and you move it up to 40 feet in any direction within the spell's range. On a successful save, it takes {@damage 8d6} Necrotic damage only.

spell.name: Phantom Panther
entries: You conjure a spectral panther that pounces on a creature you can see within range, mauling and pinning it for your allies close in for the kill.

The target makes a Strength saving throw. On a failed save, the target takes {@damage 8d6} Force damage and has the {@condition Restrained|XPHB} condition until the spell ends. On a successful save, the target takes half as much damage only, and the spell ends. While {@condition Restrained|XPHB}, the target takes {@damage 2d6} Force damage at the start of each of its turns. The target or a creature within reach of it can take an action to make a Strength ({@skill Athletics|XPHB}) check against your spell save DC. On a success, the spell ends.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
entries: The damage increases by {@dice 1d6} for each spell slot level above 3.

spell.name: Red Sign
entries: You reach above your head and conjure the dreaded Red Sign, a sigil of immense power that is inimical to all creatures. At the end of each of your turns for the duration, each creature (including yourself) within 30 feet of you that can see you takes {@damage 5d8} Necrotic damage. This damage can't break your {@status Concentration|XPHB}. If this damage reduces a creature to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, it and everything nonmagical it is wearing and carrying collapses into a pile of slime and viscera. The target can be revived only by a True Resurrection or a Wish spell.

spell.name: Shadowy Eruption
entries: A 20-foot radius {@variantrule Sphere [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Sphere} of gloom erupts from a point you choose within range as fell energies from the dimension of shadow flood the area. You select an entity to push through into this reality for a horrifying moment. When you cast this spell, choose one of the following options:

items.name: The Darkness Hides Terrors
entries: Creatures of your choice in the area must make Dexterity saving throws. A target takes {@damage 4d8} psychic damage on a failed save and must use a reaction to move as far away as it can from the point you chose, using the safest route. On a successful save, the target takes half as much damage only.

items.name: Tomb of Shadows
entries: Creatures of your choice in the area must make Constitution saving throws. A target takes {@damage 4d8} necrotic damage on a failed save and its speed is reduced by 20 feet. On a successful save, the target takes half as much damage only.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
entries: The damage increases by {@dice 1d8} for each spell slot level above 3.

spell.name: Tide of Darkness
entries: You unleash a wave of swirling shadows that fills an area next to you. Each creature in a 10-foot {@variantrule Cube [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cube} of {@variantrule Darkness|XPHB} originating from you when you cast the spell makes a Wisdom saving throw. On a failed save, a creature takes {@damage 2d8} psychic damage and is {@condition Blinded|XPHB} until it is no longer in the area of {@variantrule Darkness|XPHB}. On a successful save, it takes half as much damage and isn't {@condition Blinded|XPHB}. The {@variantrule Darkness|XPHB} lasts until the end of your next turn.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
entries: The size of the cube increases by 5 feet for each spell slot level above 1.

spell.name: Umbral Tendril
entries: You create a long tendril of life-stealing shadow that lashes out at a creature in range that you can see.
Make a ranged spell attack against the target. On a hit the target takes {@damage 2d8} Necrotic damage and you gain {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} equal to the damage dealt.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
entries: The damage increases by {@dice 1d8} for each spell slot level above 1.

spell.name: Voorish Sign
entries: You have mastered the Voorish sign, a sigil from an ancient era said to predate the world's creation. Choose a creature that you can see within range. When you would normally roll one or more dice to deal damage to that creature with a spell, don't roll those dice for the damage; instead use the highest number possible for each die. In addition, you can choose a spell of level 5 or lower that you can cast, that has a casting time of an action. You can cast that spell as part of casting Voorish Sign, expending spell slots for both.

spell.name: Wave of Oblivion
entries: You create a 30-foot {@variantrule Cone [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cone} of oblivion. Each {@variantrule Bloodied|XPHB} creature in the {@variantrule Cone [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cone} makes a Constitution saving throw. On a failed save a creature dies, and it and everything nonmagical it is wearing and carrying are turned to dust. The target can be revived only by a True Resurrection or a Wish spell.

spell.name: Summon Shadow
entries: You call forth a spirit of shadow. It manifests as a silhouette of pure blackness in an unoccupied space you can see within range. This corporeal form uses the {@creature Shadow Spirit|cthulhutorchlight} stat block. When you cast this spell, choose Infiltrator or Assassin. Your choice determines certain traits in its stat block. The creature disappears when it drops to 0 hit points or when the spell ends.

The creature is an ally to you and your companions. In combat, the creature shares your initiative count, but it takes its turn immediately after yours. It obeys your verbal commands (no action required by you). If you don't issue any, it takes the {@action Dodge|XPHB} action and uses its move to avoid danger.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
entries: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 6th level or higher, use the higher level wherever the spell's level appears in the stat block.

spellcasting.name: Spellcasting
headerEntries: The colour out of space casts one of the following spells, requiring no Material components and using Intelligence as the spellcasting ability (spell save {@dc 12}):

trait.name: Magic Resistance The colour out of space has Advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The colour out of space makes two Draining Lash attacks.

action.name: Draining Lash
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 9}, Reach 15 ft. {@h}20 ({@damage 3d8 + 6}) Necrotic damage.

action.name: Absorb Life {@recharge 5}
entries: {@actSave con} {@dc 17}, non-Mythos creatures in a 30-foot Emanation centered on the colour out of space. {@actSaveFail} 44 ({@damage 8d10}) Necrotic damage. {@actSaveSuccess} Half damage. If three or more creatures fail their saving throw against this effect, the colour out of space gains an additional action on its next turn.

action.name: At Will:
entries: {@spell Dispel Magic|PHB}, {@spell Dominate Monster|PHB}, {@spell Tongues|PHB}.

bonus.name: Horrid Burrowing
entries: The colour out of space destroys an unattended, non-magical object that it can touch that has hit points less than or equal to the colour's maximum hit points.

spellcasting.name: Spellcasting
headerEntries: The lesser crawling one casts one of the following spells, requiring no Material components and using Intelligence as the spellcasting ability (spell save {@dc 14}):

trait.name: Magic Resistance The lesser crawling one has Advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects
Relentless Evil
entries: When a lesser crawling one is reduced to 0 Hit Points, it falls into a pile of rotting flesh, worms, and maggots. If it takes at least 15 Fire damage before the end of its next turn, the lesser crawling one is permanently destroyed. It can still be targeted by spells and effects that target creatures. Otherwise, the lesser crawling one reforms in {@dice 2d6} days. The new body appears in an unoccupied space within the tomb it was originally buried in.

action.name: Slimy Grasp
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 3}, Reach 5 ft. {@h}6 ({@damage 1d8 + 1}) Necrotic damage, and a Medium or smaller creature has its Speed reduced by 10 feet until the end of its next turn as it is covered in slime and maggots.

legendary.name: By My Command
entries: The lesser crawling one chooses up to three willing Undead or Mythos creatures it can see. Each of the chosen creatures can take a Reaction to move up to its Speed, and each creature gains Advantage on the next attack roll it makes before the lesser crawling one's next turn.

legendary.name: Squirming Escape
entries: The lesser crawling one teleports up to 60 feet to an unoccupied space it can see.

spellcasting.name: Spellcasting
headerEntries: The lesser crawling one casts one of the following spells, requiring no Material components and using Intelligence as the spellcasting ability (spell save {@dc 18}).

trait.name: Magic Resistance The greater crawling one has Advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects
Implacable Evil
entries: When a greater crawling one is reduced to 0 Hit Points, it falls into a pile of rotting flesh, worms, and maggots. If it takes at least 40 Fire damage before the end of its next turn, the greater crawling one is permanently destroyed. It can still be targeted by spells and effects that target creatures. Otherwise, the greater crawling one reforms in {@dice 2d6} days. The new body appears in an unoccupied space within the tomb it was originally buried in.

action.name: Slimy Grasp
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 7}, Reach 5 ft. {@h}8 ({@damage 1d8 + 3}) Necrotic damage.

legendary.name: By My Command
entries: The greater crawling one chooses up to three willing Undead or Mythos creatures it can see. Each of the chosen creatures can take a Reaction to move up to its Speed, and each creature gains Advantage on the next attack roll it makes before the greater crawling one's next turn.

legendary.name: Squirming Escape
entries: The greater crawling one teleports up to 60 feet to an unoccupied space it can see.

trait.name: Amphibious
entries: Dagon can breathe air and water.

trait.name: Legendary Resistance (4/Day)
entries: If Dagon fails a saving throw, it can choose to succeed instead.

trait.name: One With the Sea
entries: When Dagon drops to 0 hit points or would be killed outright, it instead transforms into a swirling body of water. At the start of each of Dagon's turns, it gains one recovery point but otherwise cannot use actions or legendary actions other than Relentless Horror. When it gains its fourth recovery point, its maximum hit points return to normal if they have been reduced and it regains all lost hit points. It reforms its physical body in a space of its choice within 10 miles of its location. It then loses all recovery points.

If Dagon is the target of a {@spell Banishment|PHB} spell cast with a 9th-level spell slot while in this cloud form, it automatically fails its saving throw and is instead sent to the deepest corner of the ocean where it slumbers for {@dice 6d10} years.

trait.name: Siege Monster
entries: Dagon deals double damage to objects and structures.

trait.name: Spectral Waves
entries: Storm-tossed waves formed of magical force swirl around Dagon in a 120-foot Emanation originating from him. Creatures without a Swim speed treat this area as Difficult Terrain and take 10 force damage if they end their turn in this area. Flying creatures that end their turn in this area must make {@dc 24} Strength saving throws, gaining the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition on a failed save.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: Dagon makes four Slashing Claw attacks.

action.name: Slashing Claw
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 17}, Reach 20 ft. {@h}21 ({@damage 2d10 + 10}) Slashing damage and the target is pushed up to 10 feet away from Dagon and gains the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition.

action.name: Hungry Tide {@recharge 5}
entries: {@actSave con} {@dc 24}, creatures of Dagon's choice within 120 ft. of it; {@actSaveFail 1} The target takes 11 ({@damage 2d10}) force damage, and gains the {@condition Restrained|XPHB} condition until the end of its next turn, when it repeats the save. {@actSaveFail 2} The target takes {@dice 4d20} (22) force damage its speed is reduced to 0 and cannot be increased until it uses an action to make a successful {@dc 24} Strength ({@skill Athletics}) check.

legendary.name: Disdainful Swipe
entries: Dagon makes a Slashing Claw attack.

legendary.name: Storm Tossed Seas
entries: {@actSave str} {@dc 25}, creatures within 120 ft. of Dagon of its choice; {@actSaveFail} Dagon moves the target up to 30 feet and the target gains the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition.

legendary.name: Relentless Horror {@recharge 5}
entries: Dagon chooses one of the following options: It teleports up to 200 ft. to an unoccupied space.
It immediately ends one spell or condition affecting it.
It ends one spell or magical effect of its choice within 120 ft. of it.

spellcasting.name: Spellcasting
headerEntries: The lesser dark young of Shub-Niggurath casts one of the following spells, requiring no Material components and using Wisdom as the spellcasting ability (spell save {@dc 13}):

trait.name: Magic Resistance The lesser dark young of Shub-Niggurath has Advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The lesser dark young of Shub-Niggurath makes four Flailing Tentacle attacks. It can replace two attacks with a use of Spellcasting.

action.name: Flailing Tentacle
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 5}, Reach 20 ft. {@h}8 ({@damage 1d8 + 3}) Bludgeoning damage. If the target is a Medium or smaller creature, it has the {@condition Grappled|XPHB} condition (escape {@dc 13}).

legendary.name: Swarming Tentacles
entries: The lesser dark young of Shub-Niggurath makes two Flailing Tentacle attacks.

legendary.name: Clamor of Hooves
entries: The lesser dark young of Shub-Niggurath moves up to its Speed.

spellcasting.name: Spellcasting
headerEntries: The greater dark young of Shub-Niggurath casts one of the following spells, requiring no Material components and using Wisdom as the spellcasting ability (spell save {@dc 18}):

trait.name: Magic Resistance The greater dark young of Shub-Niggurath has Advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The greater dark young of Shub-Niggurath makes four Flailing Tentacle attacks. It can replace two attacks with a use of Spellcasting.

action.name: Flailing Tentacle
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 10}, Reach 30 ft. {@h}15 ({@damage 2d8 + 6}) Bludgeoning damage. If the target is a Large or smaller creature, it has the {@condition Grappled|XPHB} condition (escape {@dc 18}).

legendary.name: Swarming Tentacles
entries: The greater dark young of Shub-Niggurath makes two Flailing Tentacle attacks.

legendary.name: Clamor of Hooves
entries: The greater dark young of Shub-Niggurath moves up to its Speed.

trait.name: Mythos Fanaticism
entries: If the deep one hybrid can see a Mythos creature of size Large or larger, it gains a +5 bonus to Initiative rolls and gains 2 Temporary Hit Points at the start of its turn.

action.name: Trident
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 4}, Reach 5 ft. {@h}7 ({@damage 1d8 + 2}) Piercing damage.

action.name: Light Crossbow
entries: {@atkr r} {@hit 2}, range 80/320 ft. {@h}5 ({@damage 1d8}) Piercing damage.

reaction.name: My Life for Yours
entries: {@actTrigger} A Mythos creature without this Reaction is hit by a melee attack. {@actResponse} This creature becomes the target of the triggering attack if it is in range of the original attack.

spellcasting.name: Spellcasting
headerEntries: The deep one casts one of the following spells, requiring no Material components and using Intelligence as the spellcasting ability (spell save {@dc 12}):

trait.name: Amphibious
entries: The deep one can breathe air and water.
Magic Resistance The deep one has Advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The deep one makes two claw and one bite attacks.

action.name: Claw
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 5}, Reach 10 ft. {@h}6 ({@damage 1d4 + 3}) Slashing damage.

action.name: Bite
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 5}, Reach 5 ft. {@h}7 ({@damage 1d6 + 3}) Piercing damage.

action.name: At Will:
entries: {@spell Charm Person|PHB}, {@spell Disguise Self|PHB}, {@spell Message|PHB}.
1/Day: {@spell Thunderwave|PHB}.

trait.name: Dimensional Attunement
entries: A dimensional shambler gains 1 Dimension Point at the end of its turn during combat or when it is otherwise threatened. It begins an encounter with 0 points and can have up to 4.

trait.name: Dimensional Warping
entries: {@actSave wis} {@dc 14}, if a creature within 30 feet of the dimensional shambler attempts to teleport. {@actSaveFail} The creature doesn't teleport. {@actSaveSuccess} The creature teleports as normal.
Magic Resistance The dimensional shambler has Advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The dimensional shambler makes up to four Dimensional Claw attacks and uses Disdainful Banishment. It can't attack the same creature more than twice with Dimensional Claw when it uses Multiattack.

action.name: Dimensional Claw
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 8}, Reach 10 ft. {@h}11 ({@damage 1d10 + 5}) Slashing damage. If the target is a Medium or smaller creature, it has the {@condition Grappled|XPHB} condition (escape {@dc 16}). It can Grapple up to two Medium or smaller creatures at a time.

action.name: Disdainful Banishment
entries: {@actSave wis} {@dc 14}, one creature {@condition Grappled|XPHB} by the dimensional shambler. {@actSaveFail} The target is teleported up to 30 feet to an unoccupied space the dimensional shambler can see.

bonus.name: Dimensional Flurry
entries: A dimensional shambler can spend 3 Dimension Points to gain an extra use of Legendary Action. It must use the additional use before its next turn.

bonus.name: Dimensional Rend
entries: A dimensional shambler can spend 1 Dimension Point to extend its claws through space and time, increasing its Reach with Dimensional Claw to 30 feet and allowing it to ignore all cover with those attacks until the end of its turn.

bonus.name: Dimensional Step
entries: A dimensional shambler can spend 3 Dimension Points to teleport itself and all creatures it has {@condition Grappled|XPHB} to a chosen destination it has visited within the past month.

bonus.name: Teleport
entries: The dimensional shambler teleports up to 60 feet to an unoccupied space it can see.

legendary.name: Attack
entries: The dimensional shambler makes up to two Dimensional Claw attacks.

legendary.name: Shift
entries: The dimensional shambler uses Dimensional Step.

legendary.name: Step
entries: The dimensional shambler uses Teleport.

spellcasting.name: Spellcasting
headerEntries: The elder thing casts one of the following spells, requiring no Material components and using Intelligence as the spellcasting ability (spell save {@dc 15}):

trait.name: Magic Resistance The elder thing has Advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The elder thing makes up to two Grasping Tentacle attacks and uses Spellcasting.

action.name: Grasping Tentacle
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 10}, Reach 20 ft. {@h}11 ({@damage 1d8 + 6}) Bludgeoning damage. If the target is a Medium or smaller creature, it has the {@condition Grappled|XPHB} condition (escape {@dc 18}). While {@condition Grappled|XPHB} the target has the {@condition Restrained|XPHB} condition.

action.name: At Will:
entries: {@spell Charm Person|PHB}, {@spell Scorching Ray|PHB}, {@spell Thunderwave|PHB}.
1/day: {@spell Cone of Cold|PHB}, {@spell Fear|PHB}, {@spell Ice Storm|PHB}.

bonus.name: Surgical Eye
entries: The elder thing assesses the anatomy of a creature it can see within 30 ft. and makes a {@dc 15} Intelligence ({@skill Medicine}) check. If it succeeds, its attacks against the target are critical hits on die rolls of 18 or higher for the next minute or until it uses Surgical Eye against a different target. If the elder thing succeeds on this check a second time against the same creature, its attacks against the target are instead critical hits on die rolls of 16 or higher for the next minute or until it uses Surgical Eye against a different target.

legendary.name: Attack
entries: The elder thing makes a Grasping Tentacle attack.

legendary.name: Ghoulish Study
entries: The elder thing uses Surgical Eye.

legendary.name: Sudden Movement
entries: The elder thing moves up to its speed.

hp.special: 50 (Assassin only) or 40 (Infiltrator only) + 10 for each spell level above 5

trait.name: Incorporeal Passage
entries: The spirit can move through other creatures and objects as if they were {@variantrule Difficult Terrain|XPHB}. If it ends its turn inside an object, it is shunted to the nearest unoccupied space and takes {@damage 1d10} Force damage for every 5 feet traveled.

trait.name: Shadow Stealth
entries: The spirit may take the {@action Hide|XPHB} action as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} when in {@variantrule Dim Light|XPHB} or {@variantrule Darkness|XPHB}.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The spirit makes a number of attacks equal to half this spell's level (round down).

action.name: Chill Touch (Infiltrator Only)
entries: {@atkr m} {@hitYourSpellAttack Bonus equals your spell attack modifier}, Reach 5 ft. {@h}{@damage 1d8 + 3 + summonSpellLevel} Cold damage.

action.name: Deathly Bolt (Assassin Only)
entries: {@atkr r} {@hitYourSpellAttack Bonus equals your spell attack modifier}, range 150 ft. {@h}{@damage 2d4 + 3 + summonSpellLevel} Cold damage. If the spirit was hidden when it made the attack, the attack deals an additional {@damage 2d4} Cold damage.

trait.name: Magic Resistance The flying polyp has Advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The flying polyp makes up to four Grasping Tentacle attacks and uses Wind Blast (if available).

action.name: Writhing Tentacles
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 14}, Reach 20 ft. {@h}16 ({@damage 1d10 + 10}) Bludgeoning damage. If the target is a Large or smaller creature, it has the {@condition Grappled|XPHB} condition (escape {@dc 24}). While {@condition Grappled|XPHB}, the target has the {@condition Restrained|XPHB} condition.

action.name: Wind Blast {@recharge 5}
entries: {@actSave str} {@dc 24}, each creature in a 20-foot-radius {@variantrule Sphere [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Sphere} centered on a point the flying polyp can see within 120 feet. {@actSaveFail} 18 ({@damage 4d8}) Cold damage and the target is pushed up to 30 feet away from the flying polyp in a straight line. {@actSaveSuccess} Half damage.

bonus.name: Grasping Wind
entries: {@actSave str} {@dc 24}, on creature within 2,000 feet. The flying polyp must have an unblocked line between it and its target, but the line does not need to be straight. The polyp does not need to see the target. {@actSaveFail} The target's {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} is reduced to 0 and cannot be increased until the end of the flying polyp's next turn. {@actSaveSuccess} No effect.

bonus.name: Invisibility {@recharge 5}
entries: The flying polyp gains the {@condition Invisible|XPHB} condition until the end of its next turn.

legendary.name: Attack
entries: The flying polyp makes a Writhing Tentacles.

spellcasting.name: Spellcasting
headerEntries: The formless spawn casts one of the following spells, requiring no Material components and using Intelligence as the spellcasting ability (spell save {@dc 12}):

trait.name: Amorphous
entries: The formless spawn can move through a space as narrow as 1 inch without expending extra movement to do so.
Magic {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} The formless spawn has {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The formless spawn makes up to four attacks, using Bludgeoning Pseudopod or Grasping Tentacle in any combination. It can replace any number of attacks with uses of Crushing Grip or Throw Victim.

action.name: Bludgeoning Pseudopod
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 10}, Reach 10 ft. {@h}17 ({@damage 2d10 + 6}) Bludgeoning damage.

action.name: Crushing Grip
entries: {@actSave str} {@dc 18}, one creature {@condition Grappled|XPHB} by the formless spawn. {@actSaveFail} 14 ({@damage 4d6}) Bludgeoning damage. {@actSaveSuccess} Half damage.

action.name: Grasping Tentacle
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 10}, Reach 20 ft. {@h}17 ({@damage 2d10 + 6}) Slashing damage. If the target is a Medium or smaller creature, it has the {@condition Grappled|XPHB} condition (escape {@dc 18}), and it has the {@condition Restrained|XPHB} condition until the grapple ends.

action.name: Throw Victim
entries: The formless spawn throws a Medium or smaller creature {@condition Grappled|XPHB} by it to a space it can see within 30 feet of itself that isn't in the air. {@actSave dex} {@dc 18}, the creature thrown and each creature in the destination space. {@actSaveFail} 14 ({@damage 4d6}) Bludgeoning damage, and the target has the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition. {@actSaveSuccess} Half damage only

action.name: At Will:
entries: {@spell Darkness|XPHB}, {@spell Locate Object|XPHB}, {@spell Silence|XPHB}.

trait.name: Magic Resistance The gnoph-keh has Advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The gnoph-keh makes six Rending Claw attacks.

action.name: Rending Claw
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 10}, Reach 10 ft. {@h}12 ({@damage 1d8 + 7}) Slashing damage.

bonus.name: Heart of the Storm
entries: The gnoph-keh can generate a supernatural storm that fills the area around it in a howling blizzard. Driving snow and wind fill the area in a 300-foot {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} centered on the gnoph-keh. The area is {@variantrule Heavily Obscured|XPHB}, and exposed flames in the area are doused. This effect ends if the gnoph-keh has the {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} condition or dies. It can also dismiss the effect as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}.
A creature that ends its turn in this {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} takes 3 ({@damage 1d6}) Cold damage.

trait.name: Even Death May Die
entries: When Great Cthulhu drops to 0 hit points or would be killed outright, it instead transforms into a cloud of green mist that fills its space. At the start of each of Great Cthulhu's turns, it gains one recovery point but otherwise cannot use actions or legendary actions other than Relentless Horror. When it gains its fourth recovery point, its maximum hit points return to normal if they have been reduced and it regains all lost hit points. It reforms its physical body in a space of its choice within 10 miles of its location. It then loses all recovery points.

If Great Cthulhu is the target of a {@spell Banishment|XPHB} spell while in this cloud form cast with a 9th-level spell slot, it automatically fails its saving throw and is instead sent to R'lyeh where it slumbers for {@dice 10d10} years.

trait.name: Horrid Aura
entries: A non-mythos entity that starts its turn within 30 feet of Great Cthulhu takes 10 psychic damage.

trait.name: Legendary Resistance (6/Day)
entries: If Great Cthulhu fails a saving throw, it can choose to succeed instead.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: Great Cthulhu makes six Crushing Claw attacks.

action.name: Crushing Claw
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 19}, Reach 25 ft. {@h}28 ({@damage 4d8 + 10}) Bludgeoning damage and the target is either knocked {@condition Prone|XPHB} or moved up to 20 ft. in a straight line from Great Cthulhu.

action.name: Apocalyptic Majesty {@recharge 5}
entries: {@actSave wis} {@dc 23}, creatures of Great Cthulhu's choice that can see or hear it within 500 ft. {@actSaveFail 1} The target takes 110 ({@damage 20d10}) Psychic damage, and is {@condition Frightened|XPHB} of Great Cthulhu until the end of its next turn, when it repeats the save. {@actSaveFail 2} On its next turn, Great Cthulhu controls the target. This effect ends at the end of that turn.

legendary.name: Disdainful Swipe
entries: Great Cthulhu makes two Crushing Claw attacks.

legendary.name: Relentless Horror {@recharge 5}
entries: Great Cthulhu chooses one of the following options: It teleports up to 200 ft. to an unoccupied space.
It immediately ends one spell or condition affecting it.
It ends one spell or magical effect of its choice within 120 ft. of it.

legendary.name: Sudden Movement
entries: The flying polyp moves up to its speed.

spellcasting.name: Spellcasting
headerEntries: The Yithian casts one of the following spells, requiring no Material components and using Intelligence as the spellcasting ability (spell save {@dc 17}):

trait.name: Magic Resistance The Yithian has Advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects
Time Master
entries: When the Yithian rolls {@variantrule Initiative|XPHB}, it may choose to treat the result of its {@dice d20} roll as a 5 or 15. It may also apply this effect to up to six willing creatures it can see.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The Yithian makes two Snapping Claw attacks.

action.name: Snapping Claw
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 11}, Reach 15 ft. {@h}29 ({@damage 4d10 + 7}) Slashing damage.

action.name: At Will:
entries: {@spell Charm Person|XPHB}, {@spell Banishment|XPHB}, {@spell Dispel Magic|XPHB}, {@spell Fly|XPHB}, {@spell Lightning Bolt|XPHB}, {@spell Misty Step|XPHB}.
1/Day: {@spell Dominate Monster|XPHB}, {@spell Foresight|XPHB}, {@spell Meteor Swarm|XPHB}

legendary.name: Attack
entries: The Yithian makes a Snapping Claw attack.

legendary.name: Spell
entries: The Yithian uses Spellcasting, but can only cast spells it can use at will.

spellcasting.name: Spellcasting
headerEntries: The hunting horror casts one of the following spells, requiring no Material components and using Intelligence as the spellcasting ability (spell save {@dc 14}):

trait.name: Light Sensitivity
entries: While in {@variantrule Bright Light|XPHB}, the hunting horror has {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on attack rolls and checks.
Magic {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} The hunting horror has {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The hunting horror makes one Bite attack and one Tail attack, It can replace one attack with a use of Spellcasting.

action.name: Bite
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 10}, Reach 20 ft. {@h}17 ({@damage 2d10 + 6}) Piercing damage.

action.name: Tail
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 10}, Reach 20 ft. {@h}15 ({@damage 2d8 + 6}) Bludgeoning damage. If the target is a Medium or smaller creature, it has the {@condition Grappled|XPHB} condition (escape {@dc 18}).

bonus.name: Prey's Doom
entries: The horror unravels a target's life force with a bite. The hunting horror studies one creature it can see within 30 feet of itself. For 1 minute, once on each of the hunting horror's turns when it hits the target with an attack roll using its Bite attack, it can cause the target to take an extra 18 ({@damage 4d8}) Necrotic damage, and the target can't regain {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} until the end of hunting horror's next turn. On each of the hunting horror's subsequent turns, it can take a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to deal 18 ({@damage 4d8}) Necrotic damage to the target automatically.

This effect ends if the target is ever outside the range, if it has {@variantrule Cover|XPHB|Total Cover} from the hunting horror, if it is on a different plane of existence, or if hunting horror uses this {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} on a different creature.

legendary.name: Gnashing Teeth
entries: The hunting horror makes one Bite attack.

legendary.name: Crushing Horror
entries: A creature {@condition Grappled|XPHB} by the hunting horror takes 22 ({@damage 4d10}) Bludgeoning damage.

legendary.name: Pursuit of Prey
entries: The hunting horror moves up to its {@variantrule Speed|XPHB}.

spellcasting.name: Spellcasting
headerEntries: The mi-go casts one of the following spells, requiring no Material components and using Intelligence as the spellcasting ability (spell save {@dc 15}):

trait.name: Magic Resistance The mi-go has Advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects
Space Traveler
entries: The mi-go does not need to breathe air.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The mi-go makes two Claw attacks and uses Spellcasting.

action.name: Claw
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 5}, Reach 5 ft. {@h}7 ({@damage 1d8 + 2}) Piercing damage.

bonus.name: Hideous Counsel
entries: The mi-go confers with a human brain it has sealed away in a jar. The specimen is specifically chosen for the knowledge it can provide to the mi-go, allowing it to adapt to a variety of situations. The mi-go chooses one of the following benefits: It gains {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on attack rolls until the end of its turn.
It uses the Dodge action as part of this {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}.
It gains a +2 bonus to its spell save DC until the end of its turn.

spellcasting.name: Spellcasting
headerEntries: Nyarlathotep casts one of the following spells, requiring no Material components and using Intelligence as the spellcasting ability (spell save {@dc 20}):

trait.name: Defeat from Victory
entries: When Nyarlathotep drops to 0 hit points or would be killed outright, he is not slain. Instead, his body ripples with energy as it undergoes a horrific transformation. He returns to whatever strange realm he dwells in as his defeated form is torn apart by unseen forces. Mythos creatures with a combined CR of up to 20 emerge from the pile of gore, occupying the nearest, empty space. These creatures attack those who were hostile to Nyarlathotep and fight to the death.

trait.name: Legendary Resistance (4/Day)
entries: If Nyarlathotep fails a saving throw, it can choose to succeed instead.

trait.name: Magic Resistance
entries: Nyarlathotep has {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

trait.name: Poisoned Words, Rotten Soul
entries: A creature that succeeds in a Wisdom ({@skill Insight|XPHB}) check to detect one of Nyarlathotep's lies takes 66 ({@damage 12d10}) psychic damage and cannot communicate with other creatures for {@dice 3d6} minutes. A creature killed by this trait appears to have died of a heart attack or similar cause.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: Nyarlathotep makes two Rending Claw attacks and two Piercing Talon attacks.

action.name: Rending Claw
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 14}, Reach 15 ft. {@h}22 ({@damage 3d8 + 8}) Slashing damage and the target gains the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition.

action.name: Piercing Talon
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 14}, Reach 5 ft. {@h}14 ({@damage 1d10 + 8}) Piercing damage. {@condition Prone|XPHB} creatures have {@variantrule Vulnerability|XPHB} to this attack's damage.

action.name: Horrid Tongue Lash {@recharge 5}
entries: {@actSave con} {@dc 24}, creatures of Nyarlathotep's choice within 20 ft. of it; {@actSaveFail} The target takes 66 ({@damage 12d10}) acid damage. {@actSaveSuccess} Half damage.

action.name: Shape-Shift
entries: Nyarlathotep shape-shifts into a Small or Medium Humanoid, or it returns to this form. Other than his size, his game statistics are the same in each form. Any equipment he is wearing or carrying isn't transformed.

legendary.name: Swift Claw
entries: Nyarlathotep makes a Rending Claw attack.

legendary.name: Incite Delirium
entries: {@actSave wis} {@dc 20}, one creature within 120 ft. of Nyarlathotep of its choice; {@actSaveFail} The target uses its {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to make a weapon attack against a target of Nyarlathotep's choice.

legendary.name: Relentless Horror {@recharge 5}
entries: Nyarlathotep chooses one of the following options: It teleports up to 200 ft. to an unoccupied space.
It immediately ends one spell or condition affecting it.
It ends one spell or magical effect of its choice within 120 ft. of it.

trait.name: Hexing Aura
entries: Each creature of the rat-thing's choice in a 10-foot {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} originating from the rat-thing gains a +1 bonus to its spell save DC.
Magic {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} The rat-thing has {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

action.name: Bite
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 4}, Reach 5 ft. {@h}4 ({@damage 1d3 + 2}) Piercing damage.

trait.name: Amorphous
entries: The shoggoth can move through a space as narrow as 1 foot without expending extra movement to do so.

trait.name: Amphibious
entries: The shoggoth can breathe air and water.
Magic {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} The shoggoth has {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The shoggoth makes four attacks, usingHorrid Tendril or Hurled Mucous in any combination. It can't attack the same target more than once.

action.name: Horrid Tendril
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 15}, Reach 20 ft. {@h}({@dice 2d10 + 10}) Bludgeoning damage. If the target is a Large or smaller creature, it has the {@condition Grappled|XPHB} condition (escape {@dc 20}), and it has the {@condition Restrained|XPHB} condition until the grapple ends. It can Grapple up to four Large or smaller creatures at a time.

action.name: Hurled Mucous
entries: {@atkr r} {@hit 15}, range 200 ft. {@h}21 ({@damage 2d10 + 10}) Acid damage. If the target is a Large or smaller creature, it has the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition.

action.name: Slime Wave {@recharge 5}
entries: {@actSave dex} {@dc 20}, each creature in a 60-foot {@variantrule Cone [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cone}. {@actSaveFail} 44 ({@damage 8d10}) Acid damage. {@actSaveSuccess} Half damage. For 1 minute, the area within the {@variantrule Cone [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cone} is drenched with slimy acid. When a creature enters the area for the first time on a turn or ends its turn there, it takes 22 ({@damage 4d10}) Acid damage. A creature can take this damage only once per turn.

bonus.name: Horrid Digestion
entries: {@actSave con} {@dc 23}, one creature {@condition Grappled|XPHB} by the shoggoth. {@actSaveFail} The target takes 32 ({@damage 4d10 + 10}) Acid damage.

legendary.name: Thrash of Shifting Forms
entries: The shoggoth makes a Horrid Tendril or Hurled Mucous attack.

legendary.name: Bear Down Upon
entries: The shoggoth moves up to its {@variantrule Speed|XPHB}.

trait.name: Boundless Horror
entries: When Shub-Niggurath drops to 0 hit points or would be killed outright, it instead transforms into a swarm of insects that fill its space. At the start of each of Shub-Niggurath's turns, it gains one recovery point but otherwise cannot use actions or legendary actions other than Relentless Horror. When it gains its fourth recovery point, its maximum hit points return to normal if they have been reduced and it regains all lost hit points. It reforms its physical body in a space of its choice within 10 miles of its location. It then loses all recovery points.
If Shub-Niggurath's space is filled with magical fire it is banished from the world and prevented from returning for {@dice 4d10} years.

trait.name: Horrid Garden
entries: Horrid vines, slick acidic dew and covering in flesh, gnawing mouths erupt from the ground in a 120-foot {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} originating from Shub-Niggurath. The effect of this trait depends on its level. When Shub-Niggurath rolls initiative, set this trait's level to 0. The trait's level increase by 1 after each of Shub-Niggurath's turns to a maximum of 3. Use the highest AC bonus granted by this trait. Shub-Niggurath gains the benefits of this trait's current level and all lower ones. These benefits end when Shub-Niggurath is no longer threatened by hostile creatures.

trait.name: Level 0
entries: No effect.

trait.name: Level 1
entries: The ground in the {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} is {@variantrule Difficult Terrain|XPHB} for non-Plants. At the end of Shub-Niggurath's turns, including the turn in which this trait reached level 1, two awakened trees appear in open spots in the {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} of Shub-Niggurath's choice. They immediately roll initiative and aid their Dark Mother.

trait.name: Level 2
entries: A non-plant takes 15 Acid damage, or 30 Acid damage if it has the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition, when it ends its turn in the {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation}.

trait.name: Level 3
entries: Plants have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on all attack rolls made against non-Plants in the {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation}. A creature hit by a Plant's melee attack gains the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition.

trait.name: Legendary Resistance (4/Day)
entries: If Shub-Niggurath fails a saving throw, it can choose to succeed instead.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: Shub-Niggurath makes four total Grasping Tendril and Stomping Hoff attacks.

action.name: Grasping Tendril
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 17}, Reach 30 ft. {@h}21 ({@damage 2d10 + 10}) Bludgeoning damage and the target is moved to the open space closest to Shub-Niggurath.

action.name: Stomping Hoof
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 17}, Reach 10 ft. {@h}32 ({@damage 4d10 + 10}) Bludgeoning damage and the target gains the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition.

action.name: The Earth Hungers {@recharge}
entries: {@actSave str} {@dc 25}, creatures of Shub-Niggurath's choice within 200 ft. of Shub-Niggurath and the ground. {@actSaveFail} The target takes 110 ({@damage 20d10}) Bludgeoning damage, is pulled to the ground beneath it if it is flying, and gains the {@condition Prone|XPHB} and {@condition Restrained|XPHB} conditions as fibrous plant tendrils erupt from the ground. It repeats the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success. After 1 minute, it succeeds automatically. {@actSaveSuccess} Half damage, and the target is pulled to the ground beneath it if it is flying.

bonus.name: Tree Walk
entries: Shub-Niggurath may teleport to any open space, provided she ends that teleportation within 10 feet of a friendly Plant creature she can see.

legendary.name: Hungry Tendril
entries: Shub-Niggurath makes a Grasping Tendril Attack

legendary.name: Rapid Maneuver
entries: Shub-Niggurath uses its Tree Walk bonus action. It can't take this action again until the start of its next turn.

legendary.name: Relentless Horror {@recharge 5}
entries: Shub-Niggurath chooses one of the following options: It teleports up to 200 ft. to an unoccupied space.
It immediately ends one spell or condition affecting it.
It ends one spell or magical effect of its choice within 120 ft. of it.

spellcasting.name: Spellcasting
headerEntries: The lesser star-spawn of Cthulhu casts one of the following spells, requiring no Material components and using Intelligence as the spellcasting ability (spell save {@dc 13}):

trait.name: Amphibious
entries: The lesser star-spawn of Cthulhu can breathe air and water.
Magic {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} The lesser star-spawn of Cthulhu has {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The lesser star-spawn of Cthulhu makes two Crushing Fist attacks.

action.name: Crushing Fist
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 8}, Reach 15 ft. {@h}22 ({@damage 3d10 + 5}) Bludgeoning damage.

action.name: At Will:
entries: {@spell Dispel Magic|XPHB}, {@spell Fear|XPHB}, {@spell Misty Step|XPHB}, {@spell Shatter|XPHB}, {@spell Suggestion|XPHB}.

legendary.name: Flabby Claws
entries: The lesser star-spawn of Cthulhu makes one Crushing Fist attack.

legendary.name: Eldritch Contradictions
entries: The lesser star-spawn of Cthulhu uses Spellcasting.

legendary.name: Ravening for Delight
entries: The lesser star-spawn of Cthulhu moves up to its {@variantrule Speed|XPHB}.

spellcasting.name: Spellcasting
headerEntries: The greater star-spawn of Cthulhu casts one of the following spells, requiring no Material components and using Intelligence as the spellcasting ability (spell save {@dc 17}):

trait.name: Amphibious
entries: The greater star-spawn of Cthulhu can breathe air and water.
Magic {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} The greater star-spawn of Cthulhu has {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The greater star-spawn of Cthulhu makes two Crushing Fist attacks.

action.name: Crushing Fist
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 11}, Reach 20 ft. {@h}28 ({@damage 4d10 + 6}) Bludgeoning damage.

action.name: At Will:
entries: {@spell Dispel Magic|XPHB}, {@spell Fear|XPHB}, {@spell Misty Step|XPHB}, {@spell Shatter|XPHB}, {@spell Suggestion|XPHB}.
1/Day Each: {@spell Cone of Cold|XPHB}, {@spell Forcecage|XPHB}, {@spell Hold Monster|XPHB}, {@spell Wall of Force|XPHB}

legendary.name: Flabby Claws
entries: The greater star-spawn of Cthulhu makes a Crushing Fist attack.

legendary.name: Eldritch Contradictions
entries: The greater star-spawn of Cthulhu uses Spellcasting.

legendary.name: Ravening for Delight
entries: The greater star-spawn of Cthulhu moves up to its {@variantrule Speed|XPHB}.

feat.name: I Fought, I Lived
entries: You confronted a horror from beyond this world or spawned by terrible magic. It disregarded you as a puny mortal, but it learned its mistake the hard way when you sent it back to wherever it came from with good, old-fashioned muscle, courage, and a really, really large axe.
You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Defiant Strike
entries: While you are {@variantrule Bloodied|XPHB} and hit with a spell or weapon attack, you can choose to treat the attack's damage roll as having rolled the maximum damage possible. Once you use this benefit, you can't use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entries.name: Indomitable Heart
entries: If you have the {@condition Frightened|XPHB} condition at the start of your turn, roll a {@dice d20}. On a 10 or higher, you no longer have that condition.

feat.name: I Hid from the Terror
entries: That night is a blur. A strange presence, an overwhelming terror, and a confused jumble of screams mixed with faint memories of something terrible and hungry. You hid from it, finding a place too small for it to reach. The sounds of that night, and the awful aftermath you found that morning, will remain with you to the end of your days and beyond.
You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Shadowed Strike
entries: If attacking would end the {@condition Invisible|XPHB} condition on you, you can make an attack and keep that condition. You can use this benefit a number of times equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}. You regain expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entries.name: Beneath Notice
entries: When you attempt to hide from a creature that is larger than you, you can hide if you are lightly obscured or more than 30 feet away from it. You gain the benefits of being hidden against such creatures, but you do not hide from other creatures.

entries.name: Merge into Shadows
entries: Wisdom ({@skill Perception|XPHB}) checks made to detect you while you have the {@condition Invisible|XPHB} condition have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB}.

feat.name: I Pierced the Illusion
entries: You came across something strange and awful, made all the more terrible by its hidden nature. A town might have seemed innocent until it dragged unwitting visitors to their carefully tended sacrificial garden, or the family that took you in on a dark, stormy night were actually hungry cannibals. You survived because you saw their disguise and escaped or struck first.
You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Clear Senses
entries: If you have the {@condition Charmed|XPHB} condition at the start of your turn, roll a {@dice d20}. On a 10 or higher, you no longer have that condition.

entries.name: Ear for Falsehood
entries: When an NPC speaks to you, you can use this ability to determine if they are lying. You learn nothing about the truth they conceal or why they are lying, simply that there is a knowing falsehood in their statement. You can use this benefit a number of times equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}. You regain expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entries.name: Subtle Interrogation
entries: You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Insight checks made against creatures that you are speaking with.

feat.name: I Opened the Gate
entries: You didn't mean for things to turn out this way. You were curious and perhaps ignored a few signs, but it's hard to take ancient curses seriously. Musty old books say a lot of things, and most of them turn out to be mere fables. Most of them. The one book you read turned out to be the key to unleashing some ancient evil. Now it's in the world, and it's up to you to find it and send it back.
You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Delver into Forgotten Lore
entries: You learn three languages of your choice.

entries.name: Foul Arcana
entries: You can tap into a brief glimpse of the mythos to empower a spell, at the cost of overwhelming your mind with a horrifying, cosmic insight. When you cast a spell, you can give that spell's save DC a +2 bonus but you have the {@condition Paralyzed|XPHB} condition until the start of your next turn. You can use this benefit a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus. You regain expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

feat.name: I Survived to Tell the Tale
entries: You encountered something dreadful, a horrible creature or bizarre ritual that set you down the path to becoming an investigator of the strange. Your quick feet and instinct for survival carried you through that day and are your best assets.
You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Instinct to Survive
entries: When you take damage that would reduce you to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, you can immediately regain {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} equal to your level. When you use this benefit, you cannot use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entries.name: Superior Speed
entries: You gain a +10 bonus to {@variantrule Speed|XPHB}.

entries.name: Wriggle Free
entries: If you would gain the {@condition Grappled|XPHB} condition, roll a {@dice d20}. On a 10 or higher, you instead do not gain it.

subclass.name: Exalted Assembly of the Feline Court
subclassFeatures: Exalted Assembly of the Feline Court|Warlock|XPHB|Feline Court|cthulhutorchlight|3

subclassFeature.name: Exalted Assembly of the Feline Court

entries: If man could be crossed with a cat, it would improve man but deteriorate the cat.

The entities of the Cthulhu Mythos have many enemies. Not all who learn of these terrible creatures fall victim to their promises of forbidden power. Across the cosmos, a grand conspiracy consisting of the most unlikely of creatures.

A court of intelligent house cats seek to protect the world from the Mythos. Hailing from across planes, worlds, and planets, they use magic and manipulation to ensure that their centuries of work domesticating humans remains uninterrupted. For all their skill at providing food, warmth, and shelter, humans are all too prone to corruption, meddling in powers far beyond their grasp. All it takes is one fool with a forbidden book and generations of work crumbles as yet another human civilization falls to dust.
The Exalted Assembly of the Feline Court coordinates efforts to sustain and protect their servants. They forge magical pact with those who show the right mix of cunning, bravery, and etiquette.

subclassFeature.name: Feline Spells
entries: The magic of your patron ensures you always have certain spells ready; when you reach a Warlock level specified in the Feline Spells table, you thereafter always have the listed spells prepared.

entries.caption: Feline Spells
rows: {@spell Animal Friendship|XPHB}, {@spell Cat's Spring|cthulhutorchlight}, {@spell Feather Fall|XPHB}, {@spell Feline Grace|cthulhutorchlight}, {@spell Misty Step|XPHB}, {@spell Speak with Animals|XPHB}

subclassFeature.name: Feline Affinity
entries: You gain the ability to speak with the world's true nobility. You can cast {@spell Speak with Animals|XPHB} without expending a spell slot. When you cast the spell this way, the only Beasts you can verbally communicate with are cats, though most cats are reluctant to converse with a creature as simple as you.

subclassFeature.name: Feline Form
entries: Your patron takes pity on you and gives you the ability to adopt a superior, more pleasing form. As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you shape-shift into a Cat, a Tiny Beast. You stay in that form for a number of hours equal to half your Warlock level (rounded down) or until you use this feature again, have the {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} condition, or die. You can also leave the form early as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}. While in this form, you retain your personality, memories, and ability to speak, and the following rules apply:

entries.name: Game Statistics
entries: Your game statistics are replaced by the Beast's stat block, but you retain your creature type; {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}; {@variantrule Hit Point Dice|XPHB}; Intelligence, Wisdom, and Charisma scores; class features; languages; and feats. You also retain your skill and saving throw proficiencies and use your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus} for them, in addition to gaining the proficiencies of the creature. If a skill or saving throw modifier in the Beast's stat block is higher than yours, use the one in the stat block.

entries.name: Spellcasting
entries: While in your cat form, you can only casts spells that are on your Feline Spells feature. You can cast these spells without needing Material components. In addition, if you have the {@condition Invisible|XPHB} condition from the {@action Hide|XPHB} action and cast a spell that doesn't target a {@variantrule Hostile [Attitude]|XPHB|Hostile} creature, you retain the {@condition Invisible|XPHB} condition. Shapeshifting doesn't break your {@status Concentration|XPHB} or otherwise interfere with a spell you've already cast.

entries.name: Equipment
entries: You choose whether your equipment falls in your space, merges into your new form, or is worn by it. Worn equipment functions as normal, but the DM decides whether it's practical for your Tiny Beast form to wear a piece of equipment. Your equipment doesn't change size or shape to match the new form, and any equipment that the new form can't wear must either fall to the ground or merge with the form. Equipment that merges with the form has no effect while you're in that form.
You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum of once). You regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

subclassFeature.name: Vexing Distraction
entries: While in your cat form, you can pass off the most destructive, distracting, and irritating behavior as cute or even adorable.

As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} while in your cat form, choose a creature within 5 feet of you that can see and hear you. The target must succeed on a Wisdom save against your spell save DC or be affected by one of the following effects of your choice:

items.name: Annoy
entries: The target has {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on {@variantrule D20 Test|XPHB|D20 Tests} until the end of your next turn.

items.name: Confound
entries: The target's {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} is reduced to 0 until the end of your next turn.

items.name: Distract
entries: A creature of your choice has {@variantrule Cover|XPHB|Total Cover} against the target until the end of the chosen creature's next turn.
You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum of once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

subclassFeature.name: Nine Lives
entries: You are gifted with an almost inexplicable ability to survive dangerous situations. You have Nine Lives, which can be expended in the following ways:

items: If you take damage while you have 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, you can expend 1 life to not suffer a {@variantrule Death Saving Throw|XPHB} failure. If the damage is from a {@variantrule Critical Hit|XPHB}, you must expend 2 lives to not suffer {@variantrule Death Saving Throw|XPHB} failures.
If you are forced to make a {@variantrule Death Saving Throw|XPHB}, you can expend 1 life to cause yourself to succeed instead.
If you are forced to make a {@variantrule Death Saving Throw|XPHB}, you can expend 5 lives to turn the roll into a 20.
If you would die from massive damage, you can expend 1 life to be reduced to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} instead of being killed outright.
When you are reduced to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} and not killed outright, you can expend 1 life to drop to 1 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB|Hit Point} instead.
If you are targeted with an effect that causes instant death, such as a {@spell Power Word Kill|XPHB} spell, you can expend 9 lives to ignore the effect.

entries: You regain all expended lives when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

subclassFeature.name: Summon the Courtiers
entries: Having served the Feline Assembly with grace, tact, and good taste, you have earned their favor. When you take damage from a creature that is within 60 feet of you, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to call upon the Assembly's wrath. A horde of hissing, spectral cats emerges from the planar firmament and descends upon the creature that damaged you. That creature takes {@damage 5d10} Force damage and has the {@condition Blinded|XPHB} and {@condition Prone|XPHB} conditions until the end of your next turn.
Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB} unless you spend a Pact Magic spell slot (no action required) to restore your use of it.

item.name: Fingerprint Kit

items.name: Ability:
entries: Intelligence

items.name: Utilize:
entries: Reveal the fingerprint left behind on an object and sketch a copy (DC 20), or match a copied fingerprint against a humanoid creature's (DC 15).
This item allows you to capture the details of a fingerprint that has been left on an object and match them against another creature. It consists of an inkpad, parchment, and talcum powder.

item.name: Witchboard

items.name: Ability:
entries: Charisma

items.name: Utilize:
entries: Exchange messages of up to 10 words with an Ethereal creature within 30 feet (DC 15).

A witchboard is a slat of wood covered with letters, usually the Common alphabet though versions for different scripts are available. You can use it to contact Ethereal creatures, allowing limited conversations with them.
